subject: Re: No CTF estimation
body:
Hi Dimitry, 

Even by unselecting the K620 or disabled it directly on the Nvidia panel 
control, warp is still not running properly on this machine. I gave a try 
as well on smaller data used for single particle, and I still have this 
issue with the CTF calculation. The most surprising is that the GPUs are 
used by warp without any doubt. 

Cheers, 
Cyntia
 

Le jeudi 14 novembre 2019 00:52:34 UTC+11, Dimitry Tegunov a écrit :
>
> Hi Cyntia,
>
> tilt series CTF estimation in Warp is quite memory-intensive. The 
> footprint depends on the number of tilts, and the spatial frequency range 
> used for the estimation. The K620 card doesn't have enough memory for most 
> scenarios and, depending on how the GPU IDs are sorted, is the one used for 
> the estimation. You can either untick the checkbox next to that GPU in the 
> window title bar (look for the one with the least memory), or disable it in 
> the device manager (right click on "This PC" in explorer -> properties -> 
> device manager -- might require admin privileges).
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Wednesday, November 13, 2019 at 1:50:11 AM UTC+1, Cyntia wrote:
>>
>> Hi everyone! 
>>
>> Someone showed me how to use warp recently, and it worked pretty well on 
>> my tomograms!  So now I'm trying to use it on my own machine, but I have 
>> some issues. 
>> Using the same parameters and dataset as before, the CTF estimation is 
>> not working and then always estimates the defocus to 0. I tried different 
>> versions of Warp, reinstall everything and it's exactly the same.The 3 GPUs 
>> I have are recognized by Warp, but I was wondering, maybe the problem is 
>> the graphic cards?  I have two Tesla k40c and on Quadro K620, is that ok? 
>>
>> Thank you very much for your help!
>> Cyntia  
>>
>>
>>
>>
>>
